title: Reading and Use of English – Part 3
description: For questions 17–24, read the text below. Use the word given in CAPITALS at the end of some of the lines to form a word that fits in the gap in the same line.

content.text: In an era of (0) OVERWHELMING (WHELM) information, the ability to think critically is more (17) VALUABLE (VALUE) than ever. This skill allows individuals to (18) ANALYSE (ANALYSIS) arguments and identify potential biases in media reports. Without a (19) LOGICAL (LOGIC) approach, it is easy to be misled by (20) INACCURATE (ACCURATE) data or emotional appeals. Education systems are now (21) STRENGTHENING (STRONG) their focus on these cognitive abilities to prepare students for the (22) UNCERTAINTIES (CERTAIN) of the future. (23) INDEPENDENT (DEPEND) thinkers are better equipped to solve complex problems and make informed (24) JUDGEMENTS (JUDGE) in their personal and professional lives. By questioning assumptions and seeking evidence, we can develop a deeper understanding of the world around us. This process not only enhances our decision-making but also fosters a more (25) TOLERANT (TOLERATE) society where diverse viewpoints are considered with respect and clarity, ultimately leading to more effective and peaceful resolutions to global challenges.

example.text: In an era of (0) OVERWHELMING (WHELM) information...
example.explanation: Adjective required to modify 'information'; WHELM → OVERWHELMING.

questions.explanation: Adjective required after the quantifier 'more'; VALUE → VALUABLE.
Verb required after the 'to' infinitive; ANALYSIS → ANALYSE.
Adjective required to modify 'approach'; LOGIC → LOGICAL.
Adjective with a negative prefix required to match the context; ACCURATE → INACCURATE.
Present participle for continuous tense; STRONG → STRENGTHENING.
Plural noun with a negative prefix required; CERTAIN → UNCERTAINTIES.
Adjective with a negative prefix required; DEPEND → INDEPENDENT.
